pages.raw_text:
BY the last of this week the work
of consolidating the plants of the
Union and Inland laundries. which
has been in progress for three
weeks, will be entirely finished and
then the consolidated company will
be able to take care of all the work
offered. The plants are being
united in the building of the Inland
laundry, 0636 Superior street, which
has been taken over by the new
company as part of it's assets. This
building is on the river and has its
own water supply and electric light
plant. The consolidation will be
known as the Union laundry, the
capitalization of which will be
$4,000, or in other words, the com
bined capital of the two individual
plants, which was $4,000 for each
plant.


One-half OF the stock in the en-
larged Union laundry will be owned
by the labor unions of the city or
members of these unions. The other
half of the stock will be owned by
Mr.. Preston, owner of the Inland
laundry, whose old manager will
act jointly with II. Windebank. rep
resenting the union interests, in the
management of the plant.


In the consolidation of the two
plants it has been agreed that only
union help shall be employed. A
plan of sharing the profits with the
employees when the same shall ex
coed a reasonable figure on the in
vestment has been agreed upon. In
the three weeks since the CONSOLE
dation took place the new concern
has shown a nice profit, as under
the consolidation 1t is able to take
care of a large volume of business
at reduced price.


Sixty per cent of the $4,000 stock
In the old Union laundry was owned
by the local unions. the largest in
terest by an individual union being
held by the carpenters, with the
cooks and Mattresses second. The
other 40 per cent of the stock Is
owned by individual members oil
the local unions. the largest share
holder in this particular being Mr.
Windebank. who has been con
nected with the management OF the
Union laundry since June. The
amount of stock held by the individ
nal unions and union men in the
